description: Fitzpatrick skin type I-VI RGB correction offsets for hex splotch reading accuracy
methodology: Subtractive melanin correction: subtract offset from measured RGB to recover chromophore signal. Offsets derived from average melanin absorption spectrum across skin types.

types.name: Very Fair (always burns, never tans)
types.notes: Minimal melanin interference. Chromophore signal reads near-true.

types.name: Fair (usually burns, tans minimally)
types.notes: Slight warm-shift from melanin. Minor correction.

types.name: Medium (sometimes burns, tans uniformly)
types.notes: Moderate melanin absorption. Green/blue channels attenuated more than red.

types.name: Olive (rarely burns, tans easily)
types.notes: Significant melanin absorption across spectrum. Chromophore readings shifted warm.

types.name: Brown (very rarely burns, tans very easily)
types.notes: Heavy melanin absorption. UV-fluorescent chromophores recommended for higher accuracy.

types.name: Dark Brown/Black (never burns)
types.notes: Maximum melanin absorption. Visible-spectrum chromophores significantly attenuated. UV/NIR fluorescent mode strongly recommended.

visible.description: Standard RGB camera capture under ambient lighting

uvFluorescent.description: UV LED illumination + fluorescent chromophore emission capture
uvFluorescent.notes: Melanin is transparent to UV excitation wavelengths. Fluorescent emission bypasses absorption entirely.

steps: Capture reference patch of unaffected skin adjacent to splotch
Match to closest Fitzpatrick type by RGB distance
Apply correction offset to splotch RGB measurement
Calculate corrected hex value for cancer type lookup
